brent.label: Brent Crude $/bbl
brent.change: +25.3% since Feb 27 — Brent $88.96/bbl (Apr 17 close, -9.5% to -10.5%) — dated spot all-time high >$144 — HORMUZ RECLOSED APR 18 — dangerous Monday gap risk
brent.tooltip: Brent Crude: The international benchmark. Pre-war close: $71.00/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 17 close: ~$88.96/bbl — sharpest single-session drop (-9.5 to -10.5%) since Apr 8 on Iran's morning claim Hormuz was 'completely open.' Physical dated Brent traded at an all-time high above $144 against futures near $109 earlier in the week (~$35 premium) per Al Jazeera — extreme physical scarcity dislocation persists. Apr 18 Saturday: Iran reimposed Hormuz restrictions at ~05:19 ET; IRGC gunboats fired on Indian-flagged VLCC Sanmar Herald; ~20 outbound tankers reversed course. Polymarket: Hormuz-reopen-by-EoM odds collapsed from 64% to ~32% on the reclosure. Markets priced peace on Friday — diplomacy did not deliver by Saturday evening, creating asymmetric downside risk for Monday. IEA Director Birol Apr 16: Europe has 'maybe six weeks or so of jet fuel left' if strait remains disrupted — 'largest oil supply shock in history.' Trump Apr 18: 'no sticking points left'; blockade stays until 'transaction with Iran is 100% complete'; dismissed NATO-adjacent mission as 'paper tiger.' Deputy FM Khatibzadeh (Antalya): Iran 'not ready' for new face-to-face talks; 440 kg HEU transfer 'a non-starter.' Paris summit Apr 17: Macron + Starmer co-hosted 49 countries (US excluded) for Strait of Hormuz Maritime Freedom of Navigation Initiative. Only ~17 vessels transited Hormuz on reference Saturday Apr 11 vs ~130/day pre-war; first VLCC cleared Apr 14. EIA April STEO still forecasts Brent peaking at $115/bbl in Q2 2026. Source: ICE Futures Europe / Bloomberg / EIA STEO / IEA / CENTCOM / Treasury / WSJ.

wti.label: WTI Crude $/bbl
wti.change: +25.1% since Feb 27 — WTI $83.20/bbl (Apr 17 settle, -9.4% to -11.5%) — gas $4.076 (AAA Apr 17, -1.7¢ DoD, +90.9¢ YoY) — Hormuz reclosed Apr 18 — dangerous Monday gap
wti.tooltip: West Texas Intermediate: The US benchmark. Pre-war close: $66.50/bbl (Feb 27). Apr 17 settle: $83.20/bbl (-9.4 to -11.5%) per CME/NYMEX; Yahoo $82.59. The move was the sharpest single-session drop since Apr 8 on Iran's morning claim Hormuz was 'completely open' — a claim Iran reversed ~12 hours later at 05:19 ET Apr 18. AAA national average gas: $4.076/gal (Apr 17) — down 1.7¢ DoD, down 7.7¢ WoW, up 28.6¢ MoM, up 90.9¢ YoY. Diesel $5.593. State range: Oklahoma $3.44 low, California $5.88 high. EIA STEO projects April average $4.30. Jet fuel has roughly doubled since Feb 27 (+88%); Delta warned of $2B additional Q2 fuel costs. Treasury Sec Bessent Apr 15 forecast $3 gas this summer; designated 24+ people/companies/vessels in Shamkhani oil smuggling network Apr 16; extended Russian seaborne-oil sanctions waiver through May 16 Apr 17. March CPI +3.3% YoY (highest since May 2024) / +0.9% MoM (largest monthly jump since June 2022); core 2.6%; gas component +21.2% MoM. UMich preliminary April consumer sentiment 47.6 — ALL-TIME LOW in 74-year series. 10Y UST 4.24%; Fed Funds futures repriced aggressively toward December 2026 cut (prior: on-hold into 2027). SPR releases continue. EIA crude inventories -9.13M bbl vs +154K expected. Source: NYMEX / AAA / BLS / Treasury / Federal Reserve / EIA.

gas.label: US Gas Avg/Gal
gas.change: +$1.096 / +36.8% since Feb 27 — $4.076 nationally (AAA Apr 17) — California $5.89 — Oklahoma $3.27 — peaked $4.16 Apr 9 — Bessent: 'optimistic' about $3 gas by June-September
gas.tooltip: National average retail gasoline price. Pre-war: $2.98/gal (Feb 27). Current: $4.076/gal nationally (AAA Apr 17) — down from peak $4.16 on Apr 9 on peace-talk optimism but still highest level since August 2022. Most expensive state: California at $5.89; cheapest: Oklahoma at $3.27. Gas crossed $4.00/gallon Apr 2 for first time since Aug 2022. Diesel national average ~$5.43+/gallon. Jet fuel +88% since Feb 27 — Delta warned of $2B additional Q2 fuel costs. Treasury Sec Bessent: 'optimistic that sometime between June 20 and September 20, we can have $3 gas again.' Treasury Apr 16 designated 24+ people, companies, and vessels in the Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani oil smuggling network; halt on 30-day waivers on Iranian/Russian oil. March CPI (Apr 10) confirmed gasoline +21.2% MoM — largest monthly spike since CPI was first published in 1967. Headline CPI 3.3% YoY. UMich April consumer sentiment 47.6 all-time low. Iranian Parliament Speaker Ghalibaf taunted: 'Soon you'll be nostalgic for $4-$5 gas.' Source: AAA / BLS / Treasury / Quinnipiac / AEI.

sp500.label: S&P 500
sp500.tooltip: S&P 500 index. Pre-war close: 6,878.88 (Feb 27). Apr 17 RECORD CLOSE: 7,126.06 (+1.20%, +84.78 pts). Dow 49,447.43 (+1.79%, +868.71). Nasdaq RECORD: 24,468.48 (+1.52%, +365.78). Russell 2000 2,776.90 (+2.11%). VIX 17.48 (-2.56%). 10Y 4.24% (-6.8 bps). DXY 97.73 (-0.26%). Gold futures $4,879.60/oz (+1.48%). Silver $81.97 (+4.14%). Bitcoin ~$76,038-$78,000 (+0.55 to +4.5%). Week: S&P +3.6%. Defense mildly rebounded: ITA closed $233.68 (+2.03%), 1-yr total return +55.93%. Energy (XLE) extended sharp losses begun Apr 8 — Benzinga est XLE constituents face ~$76B annualized revenue hit. Airlines ripped higher: Alaska Air (ALK) +13.6% led the R1000; Delta (DAL) closed $71.72 (+2.6%) with implied ~$1.5B Q2 pretax fuel tailwind vs. consensus; JETS up double digits WoW. March CPI +3.3% YoY; gas component +21.2% MoM. UMich consumer sentiment 47.6 — all-time low in 74-year series. FactSet: 10% of S&P reported, 88% beating EPS, aggregate 10.8% above consensus; forward P/E 20.9. IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast, warned further escalation 'a close call for global recession.' Fed Funds futures repriced aggressively toward December 2026 cut (prior: on-hold into 2027). Treasury extended Russian seaborne-oil sanctions waiver through May 16. Markets priced a durable ceasefire on Friday — Iran reimposed Hormuz restrictions Saturday morning Apr 18, creating a dangerous Monday gap. Source: NYSE / CNBC / Bloomberg / FactSet / IMF / Federal Reserve / BLS.

daily_cost.label: Est. Daily US Cost
daily_cost.note: ~$850M/day — BLOCKADE DAY 6 — 16 warships, 10,000+ personnel — HORMUZ RECLOSED APR 18 — 49-NATION PARIS SUMMIT EXCLUDES US — munitions depletion approaching crisis (Tomahawk, JASSM, PAC-3)
daily_cost.tooltip: Estimated daily operational cost. Day 50: ~$850M/day — burn rate re-accelerating with blockade operations (16 warships, 10,000+ personnel) despite paused strike ops on Iran front (ceasefire ~240+ hours). Early-war burn rate ran ~$1.88-2.1B/day during the first week. Carrier strike group steady-state alone runs ~$6.5M/day per CNAS. Every $10/bbl of sustained oil elevation adds $1.3B annually to DoD fuel costs. Munitions depletion is the single most acute strategic constraint: 5,197 munitions across 35 types in first 96 hrs ($10-16B replacement bill in 4 days); Tomahawk ~1,000 fired by late March vs pre-war 3,100-4,500 inventory (Payne calculates 5 years of production needed to replenish first 16 days); JASSM/JASSM-ER 135 fired in first 96 hrs, projected full depletion by early May (RUSI); PAC-2/PAC-3 943 rounds fired in 96 hrs (~18 months of combined production), projected exhaustion mid-June (Boeing seeker critical bottleneck); Arrow interceptors projected depleted by end of March; PrSM+ATACMS 320 in first 16 days (~half of combined inventory). Structural single-source choke points at Williams (F107 turbofan), AMPAC Cedar City (ammonium perchlorate), Holston AAP Kingsport (RDX/HMX) cannot be relieved on operational timelines. CENTCOM: 10,000+ Sailors/Marines/Airmen aboard 16 warships (11 DDGs, 3 LHD/LPD/LSD, 1 CVN, 1 LCS) + aircraft enforcing blockade (Hegseth: <10% of total US naval power). USS Rafael Peralta (DDG-115) publicly credited Apr 17 with turning back a merchant vessel. Naval disposition (USNI Apr 13): Lincoln (Arabian Sea), Ford (Eastern Med, record deployment), Bush (off Namibia, transiting around Africa), Tripoli ARG/31st MEU (CENTCOM AOR). 21 vessels turned back since blockade began (up from 19 Apr 17); zero evaded. Blockade costs Iran ~$400M/day. Trump (Apr 18): blockade stays until 'transaction with Iran is 100% complete.' Hegseth: 'locked and loaded' on Iranian dual-use infrastructure, power generation, and energy industry should ceasefire collapse. Source: Pentagon / CENTCOM / USNI Fleet Tracker / FPRI / Payne Institute / RUSI / CNAS.

total_cost.label: Est. Total Cost (50 Days)
total_cost.note: ~$55.8B through Day 50 — triangulates $50-52B per CSIS/Pentagon/PWBM — PWBM projects $47B through end-April direct / $210B total economic impact — Arab country cost >$120B — Iran self-assessment $300B-$1T — May 1 War Powers deadline
total_cost.tooltip: Cumulative estimated cost since Feb 28. Internal site tally: ~$55.8B (Day 50). External estimates triangulate a direct US military expenditure of roughly $50-52B: CSIS reported $3.7B for the first 100 hours; Pentagon told Senate $11.3B for the first 6 days; $16.5B by Day 12; $18B by March 19; Penn Wharton Budget Model $35-38B direct military by April 4, projecting $47B through end-April before blockade operations began. Including indirect US economic losses, PWBM's Kent Smetters projects total cost could reach $210B. Arab country cost exceeds $120B (March 31); Iran's government assesses its own economic damage at $300B to $1 trillion. Harvard Prof. Linda Bilmes (CNBC Apr 14): 'I am certain we will reach $1 trillion' including long-term costs. Pentagon requested >$200B supplemental from White House (WaPo Mar 18); Deputy SecDef Feinberg leading a parallel ~$50B supplemental workstream; Pentagon comptroller Hurst told Congress eventual request would be 'north of $50B.' Nothing has cleared yet. Senate rejected 4th Iran War Powers Resolution 52-47 Apr 15 (Rand Paul crossing, Fetterman voting nay, Justice absent); House failed its version 213-214 Apr 16 — single-vote margin. Sen. Kaine will force weekly votes through the War Powers Act 60-day deadline, which expires May 1. Sen. Lisa Murkowski (R-AK) circulating draft Iran AUMF. FISA Section 702 reauthorization expired Apr 20 after postponed House procedural vote, creating a secondary intelligence-authority crisis. IMF cut 2026 global growth forecast, warned further escalation 'a close call for global recession.' Russia collecting $45-151B additional oil-revenue windfall per KSE Institute. Anti-war protest coalitions continue domestic rallies. Source: Pentagon / CSIS / WaPo / Penn Wharton / Harvard Bilmes / House / Senate / IMF / KSE Institute.

targets_struck.label: Targets Struck
targets_struck.note: CEASEFIRE ON IRAN FRONT HOLDS ~240 HRS — 35,500+ cumulative — LEBANON CEASEFIRE IN FORCE — Battle of Bint Jbeil consolidated — IDF 98th Division demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute Apr 16 — 380+ Hezbollah targets struck in final 24 hrs pre-truce
targets_struck.tooltip: Total targets struck by US and Israeli forces since Feb 28. CENTCOM Adm. Brad Cooper: 13,000+ strikes destroyed 'the military Iran built for over 40 years' — 155+ warships/submarines (largest navy elimination since WWII), 80% air defenses, 450 ballistic missile storage, 800 drone storage, 90% weapons factories, every submarine, 97% naval mines. DoD Mar 9 fact sheet: 5,000+ targets struck, 50 Iranian vessels damaged in first 10 days, 850+ Tomahawks fired — more than any prior campaign. No confirmed US/Israeli strikes on Iranian territory since 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~240 hours, ceasefire holds). Hegseth (Apr 17): US forces 'locked and loaded' to resume strikes on Iran's power grid if talks fail before Apr 22 ceasefire expiry. Trump (Apr 17): US-Iran deal 'very close'; claimed Iran agreed to let US 'dig up and remove Nuclear Dust.' Lebanon 10-day ceasefire took effect 5:00 PM ET Apr 16 — Lebanon saw the war's most intense 24 hours before the truce: IDF struck 380+ Hezbollah targets in southern Lebanon including an airstrike on Nabatieh + pre-dawn Hezbollah rocket barrage on Kiryat Shmona. Battle of Bint Jbeil consolidated: Brig. Gen. Guy Levy's 98th Division Egoz Unit commandos raided a Hezbollah 'combat compound' in the Aynata casbah, seized RPGs, Kalashnikovs, grenades, an anti-aircraft missile, and surveillance gear, then demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation. IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags. Levy delivered 'Bint Jbeil 2026: Our forces control the area' address from the captured stadium — same venue where Hassan Nasrallah gave his 2000 'spider's web' speech. Cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed. Hezbollah counter-claimed 39 operations including a D9 bulldozer hit by suicide drone. DM Katz: Lebanon campaign 'not yet completed.' Ceasefire MoU 6-point. IDF troops remain south of Litani. Wilson Center: Houthi tempo declining through Apr 14; no confirmed major Red Sea shipping strikes Apr 16-17. Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28. Source: CENTCOM / IDF / Pentagon / USNI / Wilson Center / ACLED / Al Jazeera / AFP / DoD.

us_kia.label: US Military Deaths
us_kia.note: 13 hostile KIA + 2 non-combat deaths — 303 WIA (CENTCOM/Pentagon per Apr 17 consolidated picture) — no new US casualties in 240 hrs of ceasefire — blockade Day 5 still not kinetically tested — 500+ Iraqi PMF attacks since Feb 28
us_kia.tooltip: US military deaths. CENTCOM/Pentagon consolidated picture (Apr 17): 13 killed in hostile action + 2 non-combat deaths, 303 WIA. Breakdown: 7 by direct enemy fire, 6 in KC-46/KC-135 crash in Iraq Mar 13. Named KIA: Sgt. Benjamin N. Pennington, SFC Noah Tietjens, Capt. Cody Khork. F-15E crew (494th Fighter Squadron, callsign 'Dude 44B') fully rescued Apr 4-5 from Zagros Mountains after 36-hour evasion. No new US casualties since ceasefire (8 PM ET Apr 7, ~240 hours). Senior US military official told House Armed Services Committee Apr 16 that Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28, and warned Iran retains 'thousands of missiles' and one-way attack drones. Blockade Day 5 (Apr 17): 13-14 vessels turned back cumulatively; Apr 15 USS Spruance (DDG-111) intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that attempted to evade the cordon after departing Bandar Abbas; CENTCOM Apr 16 imagery showed USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) helicopter operations diverting merchants in the Gulf of Oman. 10,000+ service members, 12+ ships, 100+ aircraft enforcing (Hegseth: <10% of total US naval power). No mine incidents, no missile launches at US vessels, no Iranian tanker seizures Apr 16-17. Iranian army threat Apr 16: if blockade continues 'no port in the Persian Gulf and the Arabian Sea will be safe.' Hegseth (Apr 17): 'locked and loaded' to resume strikes on Iran's power grid if talks fail before Apr 22. Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / HASC / Stars & Stripes.

us_wia.label: US Service Members WIA
us_wia.note: 303 WIA (CENTCOM/Pentagon per Apr 17 consolidated picture) — ceasefire ~240 hrs — blockade Day 5 not kinetically tested — Iran-backed militias conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28
us_wia.tooltip: US service members wounded/injured across all theaters since Feb 28. CENTCOM/Pentagon consolidated picture (Apr 17 manifest): 303 WIA. Most injuries are traumatic brain injuries from blast overpressure. Ceasefire in effect from 8 PM ET Apr 7 (~240 hours). No confirmed new WIA since ceasefire. Senior US military official told HASC Apr 16 Iran-backed Shiite militias have conducted 'hundreds' of attacks on US forces since Feb 28. Blockade Day 5: 13-14 vessels turned back cumulatively; 10,000+ service members, 12+ ships, 100+ aircraft deployed. Apr 15 USS Spruance (DDG-111) intercepted Iranian-flagged cargo ship evading cordon after departing Bandar Abbas. No mine incidents, missile launches, or Iranian tanker seizures Apr 16-17. Iranian army Apr 16: if blockade continues 'no port in the Persian Gulf and the Arabian Sea will be safe.' Source: CENTCOM / Pentagon / Military Times / HASC.

iranian_killed.label: Iranian Killed (est.)
iranian_killed.note: Forensics chief: 3,300+ (NBC/Al Jazeera) / Health Min: 2,076 FROZEN SINCE APR 7 / HRANA: 3,636 / 26,500+ injured / 115,000+ buildings damaged — ceasefire ~240 hrs — Iran accepted third-party nuclear monitoring 'in principle'
iranian_killed.tooltip: Iranian casualties — reporting frozen by Iran MoH since Apr 7 (reporting suppression). Apr 17 consolidated picture: 3,300+ killed per Iranian forensics chief (NBC/Al Jazeera); 2,076 per MoH (static since Apr 7); 3,636 per HRANA (1,701 civilians, 1,221 military, 714 unclassified); 26,500+ injured; 115,000+ buildings damaged. Iran International (Mar 31): 4,700+ security forces killed. Hengaw (Norway-based, Apr 8): 6,620 military killed. IDF claims 6,000+ IRGC killed (contested). Combined extrapolated estimate: ~9,100+. No Iranian deaths reported since ceasefire took effect Apr 7 (~240 hours). Pakistan Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir + Interior Minister Naqvi in Tehran Apr 15-16 carrying Washington's terms — Iran agreed 'in principle' to Pakistani proposal for third-party nuclear monitoring involving four countries plus the IAEA. Enrichment-moratorium gap: US seeks 20 years, Iran offers ~5, with a 10-year compromise on the table. Iran deputy FM Apr 17 rejected 'temporary' ceasefire extension, demanded comprehensive regional settlement. Islamabad talks Round 2 likely in Pakistan before Apr 22 ceasefire expiry. Bushehr NPP: no new strikes Apr 16-17 — Apr 4 projectile strike was 4th incident, killed 1 physical-protection staffer, no radiation increase; Iran submitted incidents via INFCIRC/1353 Apr 9. IAEA DG Grossi 'deeply concerned,' invoking 'Seven Indispensable Pillars.' AEOI head Eslami refuses inspections pending codified protocols. Infrastructure: 309 Iranian health facilities + 42 ambulances damaged (IRCS); 7 Iranian hospitals evacuated; 700+ educational facilities damaged; 120+ historical sites. Up to 3.2M IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12); 4.4M Afghan refugees at elevated risk. Iranian rial collapsed to ~1,544,000 IRR/USD (-42% YoY). Source: Iranian forensics chief (NBC/Al Jazeera) / Iran Health Ministry / Iran Red Crescent / HRANA / Hengaw / IDF / Pakistan Today / IAEA / UNHCR.

lebanese_killed.label: Lebanese Killed
lebanese_killed.note: 2,167-2,196 killed / 7,061 wounded (Lebanese MoPH/NNA) — 172 children, 93 health workers killed — 1.2M displaced — LEBANON 10-DAY CEASEFIRE EFFECTIVE 5:00 PM ET APR 16 — IDF 380+ targets in final 24 hrs pre-truce — Bint Jbeil consolidated
lebanese_killed.tooltip: Lebanese casualties from Israeli operations since March 2. Cumulative Apr 17 (Lebanese MoPH/NNA): 2,167-2,196 killed / 7,061 wounded; 172 children, 250+ women, 93 health workers killed. UNICEF: 600+ children killed or injured since March 2. Lebanon 10-day ceasefire took effect 5:00 PM ET Apr 16 — opening a second diplomatic front alongside the still-holding Iran ceasefire. Lebanon saw the war's most intense 24 hours before the truce: IDF struck 380+ Hezbollah targets including airstrike on Nabatieh + pre-dawn Hezbollah rocket barrage on Kiryat Shmona (fires, no casualties). Battle of Bint Jbeil consolidated: Brig. Gen. Guy Levy's 98th Division Egoz Unit commandos raided Hezbollah 'combat compound' in Aynata casbah, seized RPGs/Kalashnikovs/grenades/anti-aircraft missile/surveillance gear, demolished ~70 Hezbollah infrastructure sites in under a minute via coordinated detonation. IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags. Levy from captured stadium: 'Bint Jbeil 2026: Our forces control the area' — same venue as Nasrallah's 2000 'spider's web' speech. Cumulative Bint Jbeil toll: 100+ Hezbollah fighters killed. Hezbollah counter-claimed 39 operations including a D9 bulldozer hit by suicide drone. Netanyahu: Israel 'going to eliminate this large Hezbollah stronghold.' Ceasefire terms published via 6-point State Department MoU: extension conditional on Lebanon 'effectively demonstrating its ability to assert its sovereignty'; Israel retains self-defense rights against 'planned, imminent, or ongoing attacks'; IDF troops remain south of the Litani; IDF spokesman Adraee warned displaced not to return south of the river yet. Hezbollah is NOT a signatory and declared 'finger on the trigger.' DM Katz: Lebanon campaign 'not yet completed.' Tens of thousands of Lebanese IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite warning. WHO Lebanon: 133 attacks on healthcare, 80+ health-worker deaths, 55 primary care centers + 6 hospitals closed, 14 hospitals damaged. OCHA Lebanon Flash Appeal $308.3M through May. UNHCR: 202,477+ cross-border flows into Syria since Mar 2. UNICEF reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. VP Vance reportedly the internal administration broker of the Lebanon truce — publicly stated 'ball is in Iran's court.' Source: Lebanese MoPH/NNA / State Dept MoU / IDF / UNICEF / Al Jazeera / WHO / UNHCR / OCHA.

displaced.label: Displaced Persons
displaced.note: 4M+ regional — Iran 3.2M+ IDPs (UNHCR Mar 12, likely stale) — Lebanon >1.2M (1 in 5 Lebanese) — UNHCR 202,477+ cross-border flows into Syria since Mar 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals) — UNICEF RRM reached 188,155 IDPs in 360+ shelters
displaced.tooltip: People displaced from their homes across all fronts. Regional total: 4 million+. Iran: 3.2 million+ internally displaced (UNHCR Mar 12 — figure likely stale), compounded by severe flooding in 24 provinces. 4.4M Afghans in Iran at elevated risk; 70,000+ returns to Afghanistan by mid-March. Lebanon internal: >1.2 million displaced (~20% of population). UNHCR reports 202,477+ cross-border flows from Lebanon into Syria since March 2 (incl. 27,343 Lebanese nationals). UNICEF's Rapid Response Mechanism in Lebanon has reached 188,155 IDPs across 360+ shelters. OCHA Lebanon flash appeal seeks $308.3M through May. WHO Flash Appeal: $30M for 5 most-affected countries. WHO Lebanon: 60+ tonnes of medical supplies mobilized since Mar 18 via EU Humanitarian Airbridge. ICRC delivered its first medical shipment into Iran Apr 12 — first since war start. Lebanon ceasefire (effective 5:00 PM ET Apr 16): tens of thousands of Lebanese IDPs began moving southward Apr 17 despite IDF spokesman Adraee's warning not to return south of the Litani yet. No confirmed major outbreak; insulin stockout reported in Lebanese primary care; respiratory/eye risks from refinery strikes. Source: UNHCR / WHO / OCHA / IOM / UNICEF / IFRC / ICRC.

flights_cancelled.label: Flights Cancelled
flights_cancelled.note: 52,000+ flights — BLOCKADE DAY 5: 13-14 vessels turned back cumulatively — 40-NATION HORMUZ SUMMIT APR 17 EXCLUDES US — Iran 'lost track' of some mines — Treasury Apr 16 designated 24+ in Shamkhani network — only 2 transits observed Apr 16
flights_cancelled.tooltip: Commercial flights cancelled due to airspace closures: 52,000+ since Feb 28. Hormuz blockade Day 5 (Apr 17): CENTCOM cumulative 13-14 vessels turned back. Apr 15: USS Spruance (DDG-111) intercepted an Iranian-flagged cargo ship that attempted to evade the cordon after departing Bandar Abbas. CENTCOM Apr 16 imagery showed USS Michael Murphy (DDG-112) helicopter operations diverting merchants in the Gulf of Oman and USS Abraham Lincoln (CVN-72) operating in the Arabian Sea. New Treasury sanctions Apr 16 designated 24+ people, companies, and vessels in the Mohammad Hossein Shamkhani oil smuggling network. CENTCOM: 10,000+ service members, 12+ ships, 100+ aircraft enforcing (Hegseth: <10% of total US naval power). Pre-war Hormuz traffic averaged ~110 vessels/day; current transits below 10/day with only 2 observed Apr 16. IRGC 'Tehran Tollbooth' corridor around Larak Island continues at $2M per vessel (yuan/crypto). FM Araghchi insisted Apr 16 the strait is 'completely open.' Iranian army Apr 16: if blockade continues 'no port in the Persian Gulf and the Arabian Sea will be safe.' UK + France + ~40 nations (excluding US) held virtual 'Hormuz Summit' Apr 17 to design alternative multinational reopening task force — notable diplomatic rebuke of US approach. Iran has privately admitted to counterparts it has lost track of some mines it planted and cannot fully clear the strait. IEA April OMR: 10.1 mb/d March loss — largest in history; Hormuz 3.8 mb/d (80%+ decline). Blockade costing Iran ~$400M/day; NPC suspended petrochemical exports to prioritize domestic supply. China publicly endorsed peace-talk momentum Apr 16 while reportedly continuing BeiDou-3 navigation + YLC-8B radar support. Source: CENTCOM / Treasury / Reuters / IEA / Stars & Stripes / UN / FDD.

children_killed.label: Children Killed
children_killed.note: Iran MoH reporting frozen — HRANA Iran: 15% of 3,636 — Lebanese MoPH: 172 children killed — UNICEF Lebanon: 600+ killed or injured since Mar 2 — Pope Leo XIV from Cameroon Apr 16: 'masters of war pretend not to know...'
children_killed.tooltip: Children killed in the conflict. Iran MoH reporting frozen since Apr 7. Iran Red Crescent (Apr 10): 1,621 children among wounded. Lebanon MoPH Apr 17: 172 children killed cumulatively; UNICEF reports 600+ children killed or injured in Lebanon since March 2. Apr 8 'Black Wednesday' toll: 357 killed including ~33 children. Key atrocities: Minab school attack (Feb 28, ~175 killed including schoolchildren) — HRW called it a war crime; Lamerd sports hall (4 children); Karaj bridge (8 civilians). Iran: 700+ educational facilities damaged, 65+ destroyed; 30+ universities struck. Lebanon: IDF recovered 130+ weapons stashed in a Bint Jbeil school alongside Hezbollah flags Apr 16. Pope Leo XIV escalated from Cameroon Apr 16 (Bamenda's St. Joseph's Cathedral, 11-day Africa trip): 'The masters of war pretend not to know that it takes only a moment to destroy, yet often a lifetime is not enough to rebuild' — condemned 'leaders ravaging the world' and use of religion to justify military action. Trump retaliated Apr 16 calling Pope 'weak on crime and soft on foreign policy,' posted/deleted AI-generated image of himself as Christ, repeated false claim Leo wants Iran to possess nuclear weapons. VP Vance (Catholic convert) told Pope to 'stay out of politics.' Anti-war coalitions (50501, ANSWER, Code Pink, Palestinian Youth Movement, NIAC) continue domestic rallies. Pro-Pahlavi diaspora rallies drew 350,000 each in Toronto and Los Angeles on Feb 14, 250,000 in Munich. Hezbollah supporters rallied Beirut Apr 11 against direct Lebanese-Israeli talks. Source: UNICEF / Iran MoH / HRANA / Lebanese MoPH / WHO / HRW / Vatican / Hengaw / NBC.
